home.title: Find your people. Build together.
home.subtitle: The collaboration network for advisers — discover specialists, form specialty groups, and co-create the tools you use with clients.

home.discoverDesc: Find advisers and specialty groups across the network.
home.messagesDesc: Reach out, get replies, and start collaborating.
home.connectionsDesc: Your network and the groups you're in.
home.connectingDesc: Your connections and conversations, together in one place.
home.marketDesc: Buy and license tools other groups have built.

discover.title: Search the network two ways — find people and groups, and let them find you.
body: Discovery is two-sided: you advertise your strengths on your Profile so others find you, and you search outward here.
Switch between People and Groups with the tabs, and narrow results by country, service line, specialty, or ‘Available only’.
Type a specialty, industry, or name and press Search — or tap 🎤 to speak instead of type.
On a group: View it or Request to join. On a person: Reach out with a purposeful first message (say why you're contacting them and what you're asking).

connections.title: Your 1:1 connections and group memberships, all in one place.
body: Connecting is mutual — a request becomes a connection only once both people accept.
‘Requests to connect’ — people who want to connect with you; Accept or Decline.
‘Your connections’ — advisers you're already linked with. ‘Requests you've sent’ — awaiting their reply.
‘Your groups’ — the specialty groups you belong to.

connecting.title: Your connections and conversations, together in one place.
body: ‘Connecting’ brings your people and your chats into a single list — the noun (who you know) and the verb (staying in touch).
Filter with the tabs — All, Chats, Groups, Connections, Requests — and search to find anyone in a large network.
‘Requests’ holds both connection requests and group invitations — anything waiting for your decision.
Open any row to view or start the conversation.

marketplace.title: License tools and models other groups have built — deal direct, no Advisory fee.
body: Browse tools groups have created; ‘Get it’ to license one for use with your own clients.
Your licence is unlimited-client usage — use it with as many of your own clients as you like, but you can't resell or redistribute it.
Ownership stays with the group that built it: you're buying usage, not ownership.
Purchases include ongoing updates — when the group improves a tool, you get the new version.
Members pay each other directly; Advisory takes no fee and only records that a deal happened.

profile.title: Your advertised identity — this is what makes you findable on the network.
body: Your name, title, firm and contact details come from Advisory and are read-only here.
Everything else is what you advertise — turn on ‘Open to collaborate’ so groups may approach you.
Add your strengths, the industries you know, and your topics/projects; these power the searches that find you.
Write a short ‘About me’, then Save profile. Tap 🎤 to dictate any field instead of typing.

messages.title: Requests and chats — with built-in translation across languages.
body: New purposeful outreaches land under ‘Requests’ — Accept to open a thread, or Skip to dismiss.
‘Chats’ are your ongoing conversations with people and groups.
You stay in control — accept, ignore, or block; a sender gets just one outreach until you reply.
Each message keeps its original language; turn on Auto-translate to read and reply in yours.

groupNew.title: Create a specialty group — your advert to recruit collaborators.
body: A group advertises itself and recruits members; the actual work happens in its space once it's formed.
Give it a clear name, pick one emoji icon, and describe what you're doing and who you're looking for.
Add specialty tags so the right people can find it in Discover.
Choose Listed (anyone can find it) or Unlisted (invite only), then Create group. Anyone except clients can create one.

groupDetail.title: A group's advert — what it's doing and how to join.
body: See who created it, its firms and members, what they're building, and its specialty tags.
‘Request to join’ — membership is always opt-in, so the group owner reviews your request.
‘Message the group’ to start a conversation with its members.
